name: COVID-19 in human serum using Raman spectroscopy
description: Raman spectra of blood serum from 20 subjects (10 RT-PCR/ELISA-confirmed COVID-19 patients and 10 healthy controls) collected under Ethics Committee protocol 26691419.6.0000.5492 (Universidade Anhembi Morumbi). Acquired with a Dimension P1 dispersive Raman spectrometer (Lambda Solutions), 830 nm excitation, 350 mW, 400–1800 cm⁻¹, 4 cm⁻¹ resolution, 30 s acquisition (3 s × 10 accumulations), 80 µL serum per measurement, triplicate per sample. Preprocessed with 7th-order polynomial baseline correction and area normalisation (1-norm). Published PCA-DA/PLS-DA model achieved 87% sensitivity and 100% specificity.
citeAs: @article{Goulart_2022, title={Diagnosing COVID-19 in human serum using Raman spectroscopy}, volume={37}, ISSN={1435-604X}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1007/s10103-021-03488-7}, DOI={10.1007/s10103-021-03488-7}, number={4}, journal={Lasers in Medical Science}, publisher={Springer Science and Business Media LLC}, author={Goulart, Ana Cristina Castro and Silveira, Landulfo and Carvalho, Henrique Cunha and Dorta, Cristiane Bissoli and Pacheco, Marcos Tadeu T. and Zangaro, Renato Amaro}, year={2022}, month=jan, pages={2217--2226}}
citation: Goulart, Ana Cristina Castro, et al. 'Diagnosing COVID-19 in human serum using Raman spectroscopy.' Lasers in Medical Science 37.4 (2022): 2217-2226.

field.description: Sequential integer index identifying each spectrum.

field.name: raman_shift_cm_inv
field.description: Raman shift wavenumber axis in cm⁻¹. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,).

field.name: spectrum_intensity
field.description: Raman scattering intensity in arbitrary units. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,) per spectrum.

field.name: target
field.description: Class label: string identifier of the sample class.

rai:dataCollection: Dispersive Raman spectroscopy of blood serum. Dimension P1 spectrometer (Lambda Solutions), 830 nm excitation, 350 mW laser power, 400-1800 cm-1 spectral window, 4 cm-1 resolution, 30 s total acquisition (3 s x 10 accumulations), 80 µL serum in aluminium holder. Three replicate spectra per sample.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: 7th-order polynomial baseline correction applied to raw spectra to remove fluorescence background; spectra subsequently normalised by area (1-norm). Processed spectra used for PCA-DA and PLS-DA classification in Chemoface software.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Binary COVID-19 labels (positive/negative) assigned by RT-PCR confirmation and ELISA serology. 20 subjects (10 RT-PCR-confirmed COVID-19 patients and 10 healthy controls. Study conducted under Ethics Committee protocol 26691419.6.0000.5492 (Universidade Anhembi Morumbi). Each sample measured in triplicate at three anatomically distinct serum positions.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: Spectra are derived from identified COVID-19 patients and healthy volunteers. No direct identifiers are included in the released dataset, but the clinical context means data should be treated as sensitive health information under applicable data-protection regulations (e.g., LGPD in Brazil, GDPR in the EU). applicable data-protection regulations.
rai:dataBiases: Small cohort (20 subjects) limits statistical power; results may not generalise beyond this Brazilian clinical population. Equal-sized COVID-19 and control groups simplify analysis but may not reflect true disease prevalence. Serum spectra are sensitive to sample handling and storage conditions not fully controlled across sites. Instrument-specific fluorescence background (830 nm excitation) and correction method (7th-order polynomial baseline) may differ from other published COVID-19 serum datasets.
rai:dataLimitations: Twenty-subject pilot study; published discriminant model achieved 87% sensitivity and 100% specificity using PCA-DA/PLS-DA (Chemoface, PC2+PC4+PC5+PC6). Performance on independent cohorts, different instruments, or other SARS-CoV-2 variants is unknown. Access requires direct contact with the authors; the dataset is not available via an open API. No explicit open license was stated; CC BY 4.0 has been assumed — users should verify terms with the original authors.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Demonstrates feasibility of rapid, low-cost COVID-19 screening from serum Raman spectra. Potential value in resource-limited settings where RT-PCR capacity is constrained. Clinical deployment requires large-scale validation and regulatory approval; findings must not be used for diagnostic decisions without qualified medical oversight.
rai:dataUseCases: COVID-19 binary classification, serum biomarker discovery, PCA/PLS-DA benchmarking, Raman-based infectious-disease screening research.
prov:wasGeneratedBy: Data collection: Laboratory measurements on human biological samples (blood serum, saliva, tissue biopsies) collected under clinical or research protocols. Preprocessing: Spectra are provided with class labels. Any preprocessing applied (baseline correction, smoothing, normalisation) is described in the source publication. Annotation: Class labels assigned based on known sample identity or validated analytical reference methods.
